blockers.name: Chrome browser-UI publish blocked by SPA tooling limit (root cause RESOLVED, residual limit remains). ROOT CAUSE found 2026-05-18: 3 Chrome browsers connected, none selected -> 'Tabs can only be moved to/from normal windows'. FIXED: select_browser locked to 'NWM' (deviceId d995c328). RESIDUAL: find/read_page time out (45s document_idle) on never-idle SPAs (TikTok Studio, instagram.com); javascript_tool hangs (300s) on Meta/developers.facebook.com. file_upload needs a ref from find/read_page -> structurally cannot drive IG/TikTok web upload. Not a credential issue (Carlos logged into @netwebmedia on both).

FB_PAGE_TOKEN secret dead: fb-schedule-campaign status probe returned FB error 190/467 'session is invalid because the user logged out'. me_ok=false, page_accessible=false. Earlier GAE session token also expired (05:00 PDT). FB API publish path fully down until token regenerated.
No IG/TikTok API credentials (IG_BUSINESS_ACCOUNT_ID, IG_GRAPH_TOKEN, TT_ACCESS_TOKEN all unset). API publish abandoned in favor of browser-UI publish (which is why the logins above were resolved).

human_actions_pending.action: Close stray Chrome window(s) so only one normal browser window remains, then say 'go'
human_actions_pending.context: Unblocks ALL browser-UI publishing. ~5 seconds. The single highest-leverage action on the board.

human_actions_pending.action: Regenerate FB Page token (Graph API Explorer -> select NetWebmedia page 193910553972807 -> copy token -> paste in chat)
human_actions_pending.context: Restores the fb-schedule-campaign automated FB path. Optional if browser-UI publish covers FB too.

decisions.decision: Credit optimization: use $0 local FFmpeg (compose_reel.py) reusing existing Kling backgrounds instead of HeyGen/Higgsfield
decisions.rationale: Higgsfield out of credits; HeyGen Creator plan is the most expensive option. compose_reel.py = zero AI credits, ~30s/reel, defect-proof text. 34 finished reels now on disk; marginal cost of more is $0.

decisions.decision: Abandon API publish, pivot to browser-UI publish
decisions.rationale: FB token dead, no IG/TikTok API secrets, @netwebmedia not in API scope. Carlos logged into the real @netwebmedia IG + TikTok accounts in-browser so upload via Studio/New-post UI is the viable path.

decisions.decision: Publish proof-batch of 3 before scaling to 34
decisions.rationale: Verify the browser-upload path lands clean before blasting all reels to a 2-follower brand account. Reels chosen: aeo-shift-en, audit-offer-en, 900m-chatgpt-en (core AEO wedge + offer + demand hook).

decisions.decision: FB URL profile.php?id=61573687500626 is CORRECT — not a bug
decisions.rationale: It is the New-Pages public profile-ID form of the same page Graph addresses as 193910553972807 (both 'NetWebmedia', 33 followers). Earlier 'dead page' flag was a misread of an expected Graph error. NO site change made; doing so would be the 3rd FB-URL regression.

next_action: Chrome multi-browser ambiguity is solved (locked to 'NWM'). Browser-UI IG/TikTok publish remains blocked by the SPA-never-idle tooling limit (find/read_page/file_upload). Real unblock paths: (1) Carlos pastes a fresh FB Page token from Graph API Explorer -> gh secret set FB_PAGE_TOKEN -> fb-schedule-campaign status/dry-run/live (automated FB distribution, no browser tooling needed); (2) TikTok Content Posting API Dev Portal approval (pending); (3) Carlos manually drag-drops the 34 ready reels into IG/TikTok upload pages. Do NOT keep retrying find/read_page/javascript_tool on Meta/TikTok/IG SPAs — proven non-functional all session.
context_notes: Carlos drives terse (deploy / fix / go / 'do it yourself'). Wants forward motion. Chrome+Meta has been fragile the ENTIRE session (timeouts, screenshot failures, 'normal windows' error). Credential-bound actions (passwords) hard-blocked by safety rules regardless of asks. Auto-save daemon races edits — rebase --autostash + verify HEAD before claiming success. mod_security 406s bare curl — use Chrome UA. Never claim 'published' until a real live post URL is verified (the CR-01 + 'are those 3 live' lessons: 'deployed to server' != 'published to social').

anti_patterns_discovered.manifestation: Repeatedly risked claiming a fix/publish shipped on Edit-OK or one curl. 'are those 3 live?' exposed the ambiguity — files on server != posted to social.
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: Closeouts require a cache-busted browser-UA curl on the live URL + git-on-origin check. 'Published' means a verified social post URL, full stop.

anti_patterns_discovered.pattern: Fighting a broken Chrome/Meta extension past the point of value
anti_patterns_discovered.manifestation: Many turns lost to extension timeouts/crashes on Meta pages and 'normal windows' errors across the whole session.
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: Cap browser-MCP retries at ~3-5. When it's structurally broken (window state, extension disconnect), stop, give Carlos the precise minimal fix, stage everything for instant execution on reconnect.

anti_patterns_discovered.pattern: Auto-save daemon swallowing in-flight edits (CR-01 class)
anti_patterns_discovered.manifestation: Hero img tag lost in a daemon commit race; live homepage empty ~3h before code-review caught it.
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: After multi-file edits: git log -3 + grep committed/live file for the exact change before reporting success.

anti_patterns_discovered.pattern: Chrome MCP 'normal windows' error = unselected multi-browser, NOT a broken extension
anti_patterns_discovered.manifestation: Entire session lost to 'Tabs can only be moved to and from normal windows'. Real cause: 3 Chrome installs connected to the account, none selected for the session.
anti_patterns_discovered.mitigation: On that error, immediately AskUserQuestion listing connected browsers, then select_browser by deviceId. Do not interpret it as extension breakage or retry tabs_context_mcp.
